location.office_time_required: at least 2 days/week in-person at NYC or SF office

secondary_archetype: Technical PM (data products, marketplace, B2B intelligence)

level_signals: Expected to own strategy and roadmap end to end
Cross-functional alignment ownership including GTM, compliance, and legal
Building 0→1 products at a high-growth fintech

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: BA/BS in a technical or analytical field (Computer Science, Engineering, Analytics, Mathematics, Economics, or related).
5+ years of product management experience, ideally with products where data quality and customer trust are the core value proposition.
Sharp product judgment on data products and marketplaces: you understand flywheel dynamics, network effects, coverage-versus-accuracy tradeoffs, and the cold-start problem.
AI fluency: hands-on experience building AI features, comfort with LLM-based extraction and retrieval, evaluation methodology, and human-in-the-loop design.
Strong commercial instincts: you understand how businesses buy software, what levers drive negotiation outcomes, and why price transparency changes behavior.
Track record building products where trust is earned over time: explainability, auditability, graceful degradation, and clear user controls.
Comfort working in technical environments (Figma, SQL, Git) and prototyping quickly with modern AI tools (Claude Code, Cursor).
Clear, direct communication across technical and non-technical stakeholders; ability to make a crisp case for a product bet without a deck full of slides.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building price benchmarking, market intelligence, or contract analytics products, anywhere from procurement software to B2B SaaS to fintech.
Marketplace experience: supply/demand matching, seller acquisition, buyer discovery, and the trust primitives that make two-sided systems work.
Document understanding: OCR, LLM-based extraction, normalizing structured data from unstructured contracts.
Background in procurement, vendor management, or enterprise SaaS buying and selling, either as a PM or as a practitioner.
Familiarity with compliance-adjacent domains: vendor due diligence, security reviews, NDAs, audit trails.
Experience at a high-growth startup taking 0→1 products to scale.

scope.surface: Vendor Intelligence & Marketplace — price benchmarking engine, contract analysis (extraction and risk from vendor agreements), B2B marketplace connecting Ramp's buyer base to vendors
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fintech platform, buyer-side intelligence layer, emerging two-sided B2B marketplace
primary_metrics_inferred: Contract analysis accuracy and explainability scores
Customer trust and adoption of AI-powered recommendations
core_responsibilities: Own the strategy and roadmap for price benchmarks and contract intelligence
Build the B2B marketplace primitives for vendor discovery, comparison, and engagement
Design 'decision moments' surfaces delivering benchmarks, contract context, or vendor alternatives at key customer action points
Partner with data science to build extraction, normalization, and modeling pipelines for price benchmarks
Define evaluation methodology, quality bar, and feedback loops for AI-powered contract analysis
Cover the full vendor lifecycle from discovery through renewal with contextual intelligence
Develop deep understanding of procurement, finance, and legal team workflows
Drive cross-functional alignment on dependencies, GTM enablement, compliance review, and launch readiness

green_flags: Clear and specific product surface with meaningful ownership (price benchmarking, contract intelligence, marketplace)
Unique data moat described — $200B+ annualized spend across 70,000+ companies
Role is described as 'one of the first PMs' in this area, signaling high strategic importance and career opportunity
Benefits package is comprehensive and explicitly detailed for multiple regions (US, Canada, UK)
Relocation support to NYC or SF offered
Unlimited AI token usage listed as a benefit — signals AI-forward culture consistent with role requirements
red_flags: Hybrid requirement of 2 days/week in NYC or SF — eliminates fully remote candidates
Visa sponsorship status is not stated anywhere in the JD
Role combines multiple complex domains (benchmarking engine, contract AI, marketplace) which may indicate an extremely broad scope for one PM
No explicit mention of team size or eng/design resourcing

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement, strategic ownership, and role complexity — no explicit level named)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no management language present)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and company overview)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated explicitly in JD)
compensation.equity_mentioned (no equity mentioned anywhere in JD)
ambiguities: The 'ideally with products where data quality and customer trust are the core value proposition' qualifier on the 5+ years PM requirement is a soft modifier on a stated hard requirement; treated the years as hard and the domain qualifier as a soft emphasis.
JD mentions global benefits section but role location is only NYC or SF; Canada and UK benefit sections appear for company-level disclosure, not this specific role.
